NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. To Jeduthun. A Psalm of David. I said, “I will guard my ways, Lest I sin with my tongue; I will restrain my mouth with a muzzle, While the wicked are before me.”
NKJV.2: I was mute with silence, I held my peace even from good; And my sorrow was stirred up.
NKJV.3: My heart was hot within me; While I was musing, the fire burned. Then I spoke with my tongue:
NKJV.4: “LORD, make me to know my end, And what is the measure of my days, That I may know how frail I am.
NKJV.5: Indeed, You have made my days as handbreadths, And my age is as nothing before You; Certainly every man at his best state is but vapor. Selah
NKJV.6: Surely every man walks about like a shadow; Surely they busy themselves in vain; He heaps up riches, And does not know who will gather them.
NKJV.7: “And now, Lord, what do I wait for? My hope is in You.
NKJV.8: Deliver me from all my transgressions; Do not make me the reproach of the foolish.
NKJV.9: I was mute, I did not open my mouth, Because it was You who did it.
NKJV.10: Remove Your plague from me; I am consumed by the blow of Your hand.
NKJV.11: When with rebukes You correct man for iniquity, You make his beauty melt away like a moth; Surely every man is vapor. Selah
NKJV.12: “Hear my prayer, O LORD, And give ear to my cry; Do not be silent at my tears; For I am a stranger with You, A sojourner, as all my fathers were.
NKJV.13: Remove Your gaze from me, that I may regain strength, Before I go away and am no more.”

I said<S>559</S>, I will take heed<S>8104</S> to my ways<S>1870</S>, that I sin<S>2398</S> not with my tongue<S>3956</S>: I will keep<S>8104</S> my mouth<S>6310</S> with a bridle<S>4269</S>, while the wicked<S>7563</S> is before me. <sup>my mouth: Heb. a bridle, or, muzzle for my mouth</sup>

KJV.5: Behold, thou hast made<S>5414</S> my days<S>3117</S> as an handbreadth<S>2947</S>; and mine age<S>2465</S> is as nothing before thee: verily every<S>3605</S> man<S>120</S> at his best state<S>5324</S> is altogether<S>3605</S> vanity<S>1892</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>at: Heb. settled</sup>
KJV.6: Surely every man<S>376</S> walketh<S>1980</S> in a vain shew<S>6754</S>: surely they are disquieted<S>1993</S> in vain<S>1892</S>: he heapeth up<S>6651</S> riches, and knoweth<S>3045</S> not who shall gather<S>622</S> them. <sup>a vain: Heb. an image</sup>
KJV.7: And now, Lord<S>136</S>, what wait<S>6960</S> I for? my hope<S>8431</S> is in thee.

KJV.9: I was dumb<S>481</S>, I opened<S>6605</S> not my mouth<S>6310</S>; because thou didst<S>6213</S> it.

KJV.11: When thou with rebukes<S>8433</S> dost correct<S>3256</S> man<S>376</S> for iniquity<S>5771</S>, thou makest his beauty<S>2530</S> to consume away<S>4529</S> like a moth<S>6211</S>: surely every man<S>120</S> is vanity<S>1892</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>his: Heb. that which is to be desired in him to melt away</sup>
KJV.12: Hear<S>8085</S> my prayer<S>8605</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, and give ear<S>238</S> unto my cry<S>7775</S>; hold not thy peace<S>2790</S> at my tears<S>1832</S>: for I am a stranger<S>1616</S> with thee, and a sojourner<S>8453</S>, as all my fathers<S>1</S> were.
KJV.13: O spare<S>8159</S> me, that I may recover strength<S>1082</S>, before I go hence<S>3212</S>, and be no more.

ESV.1: I said, “I will guard my ways, that I may not sin with my tongue; I will guard my mouth with a muzzle, so long as the wicked are in my presence.”
ESV.2: I was mute and silent; I held my peace to no avail, and my distress grew worse.
ESV.3: My heart became hot within me. As I mused, the fire burned; then I spoke with my tongue:
ESV.4: “O LORD, make me know my end and what is the measure of my days; let me know how fleeting I am!
ESV.5: Behold, you have made my days a few handbreadths, and my lifetime is as nothing before you. Surely all mankind stands as a mere breath! Selah
ESV.6: Surely a man goes about as a shadow! Surely for nothing  they are in turmoil; man heaps up wealth and does not know who will gather!
ESV.7: “And now, O Lord, for what do I wait? My hope is in you.
ESV.8: Deliver me from all my transgressions. Do not make me the scorn of the fool!
ESV.9: I am mute; I do not open my mouth, for it is you who have done it.
ESV.10: Remove your stroke from me; I am spent by the hostility of your hand.
ESV.11: When you discipline a man with rebukes for sin, you consume like a moth what is dear to him; surely all mankind is a mere breath! Selah
ESV.12: “Hear my prayer, O LORD, and give ear to my cry; hold not your peace at my tears! For I am a sojourner with you, a guest, like all my fathers.
ESV.13: Look away from me, that I may smile again, before I depart and am no more!”

NASB.1: For the choir director, for Jeduthun. A Psalm of David. I said, 'I will guard my ways That I may not sin with my tongue; I will guard my mouth as with a muzzle While the wicked are in my presence.'
NASB.2: I was mute and silent, I refrained [even] from good, And my sorrow grew worse.
NASB.3: My heart was hot within me, While I was musing the fire burned; [Then] I spoke with my tongue:
NASB.4: 'LORD, make me to know my end And what is the extent of my days; Let me know how transient I am.
NASB.5: 'Behold, You have made my days [as] handbreadths, And my lifetime as nothing in Your sight; Surely every man at his best is a mere breath. Selah.
NASB.6: 'Surely every man walks about as a phantom; Surely they make an uproar for nothing; He amasses [riches] and does not know who will gather them.
NASB.7: 'And now, Lord, for what do I wait? My hope is in You.
NASB.8: 'Deliver me from all my transgressions; Make me not the reproach of the foolish.
NASB.9: 'I have become mute, I do not open my mouth, Because it is You who have done [it].
NASB.10: 'Remove Your plague from me; Because of the opposition of Your hand I am perishing.
NASB.11: 'With reproofs You chasten a man for iniquity; You consume as a moth what is precious to him; Surely every man is a mere breath. Selah.
NASB.12: 'Hear my prayer, O LORD, and give ear to my cry; Do not be silent at my tears; For I am a stranger with You, A sojourner like all my fathers.
NASB.13: 'Turn Your gaze away from me, that I may smile [again] Before I depart and am no more.'

NLT.1:
I said to myself, “I will watch what I do
and not sin in what I say.
I will hold my tongue
when the ungodly are around me.”
NLT.2:
But as I stood there in silence —
not even speaking of good things —
the turmoil within me grew worse.
NLT.3:
The more I thought about it,
the hotter I got,
igniting a fire of words:
NLT.4:
“LORD, remind me how brief my time on earth will be.
Remind me that my days are numbered —
how fleeting my life is.
NLT.5:
You have made my life no longer than the width of my hand.
My entire lifetime is just a moment to you;
at best, each of us is but a breath.” Interlude
NLT.6:
We are merely moving shadows,
and all our busy rushing ends in nothing.
We heap up wealth,
not knowing who will spend it.
NLT.7:
And so, Lord, where do I put my hope?
My only hope is in you.
NLT.8:
Rescue me from my rebellion.
Do not let fools mock me.
NLT.9:
I am silent before you; I won’t say a word,
for my punishment is from you.
NLT.10:
But please stop striking me!
I am exhausted by the blows from your hand.
NLT.11:
When you discipline us for our sins,
you consume like a moth what is precious to us.
Each of us is but a breath. Interlude
NLT.12:
Hear my prayer, O LORD!
Listen to my cries for help!
Don’t ignore my tears.
For I am your guest —
a traveler passing through,
as my ancestors were before me.
NLT.13:
Leave me alone so I can smile again
before I am gone and exist no more.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. For Jeduthun. A Psalm by David. I said, “I will watch my ways, so that I don’t sin with my tongue. I will keep my mouth with a bridle while the wicked is before me.”
WEB.2: I was mute with silence. I held my peace, even from good. My sorrow was stirred.
WEB.3: My heart was hot within me. While I meditated, the fire burned. I spoke with my tongue:
WEB.4: “Yahweh, show me my end, what is the measure of my days. Let me know how frail I am.
WEB.5: Behold, you have made my days hand widths. My lifetime is as nothing before you. Surely every man stands as a breath.” Selah.
WEB.6: “Surely every man walks like a shadow. Surely they busy themselves in vain. He heaps up, and doesn’t know who shall gather.
WEB.7: Now, Lord, what do I wait for? My hope is in you.
WEB.8: Deliver me from all my transgressions. Don’t make me the reproach of the foolish.
WEB.9: I was mute. I didn’t open my mouth, because you did it.
WEB.10: Remove your scourge away from me. I am overcome by the blow of your hand.
WEB.11: When you rebuke and correct man for iniquity, You consume his wealth like a moth. Surely every man is but a breath.” Selah.
WEB.12: “Hear my prayer, Yahweh, and give ear to my cry. Don’t be silent at my tears. For I am a stranger with you, a foreigner, as all my fathers were.
WEB.13: Oh spare me, that I may recover strength, before I go away, and exist no more.”

CSB17.1:
For the choir director, for Jeduthun. A psalm of David. [1] I said, “I will guard my ways
so that I may not sin with my tongue;
I will guard my mouth with a muzzle
as long as the wicked are in my presence.” ⓔ
CSB17.2:
I was speechless and quiet;
I kept silent, even from speaking good,
and my pain intensified. ⓕ
CSB17.3:
My heart grew hot within me;
as I mused, a fire burned. ⓖ
I spoke with my tongue:
CSB17.4:
“LORD, make me aware of my end
and the number of my days
so that I will know how short-lived I am. ⓗ
CSB17.5:
In fact, you have made my days just inches long,
and my life span is as nothing to you.
Yes, every human being stands as only a vapor. ⓘ Selah
CSB17.6:
Yes, a person goes about like a mere shadow.
Indeed, they rush around in vain,
gathering possessions
without knowing who will get them. ⓙ
CSB17.7:
“Now, Lord, what do I wait for?
My hope is in you. ⓚ
CSB17.8:
Rescue me from all my transgressions;
do not make me the taunt of fools. ⓛ
CSB17.9:
I am speechless; I do not open my mouth
because of what you have done. ⓜ
CSB17.10:
Remove your torment from me.
Because of the force of your hand I am finished. ⓝ
CSB17.11:
You discipline a person with punishment for iniquity,
consuming like a moth what is precious to him; ⓐ
yes, every human being is only a vapor. ⓑ Selah
CSB17.12:
“Hear my prayer, LORD,
and listen to my cry for help;
do not be silent at my tears. ⓒ
For I am here with you as an alien,
a temporary resident like all my ancestors. ⓓ
CSB17.13:
Turn your angry gaze from me
so that I may be cheered up
before I die and am gone.” ⓔ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; for Jeduthun. A Psalm of David. I said, "I will guard my ways
That I may not sin with my tongue;
I will muzzle my mouth
While the wicked are in my presence."
AMP.2:
I was mute and silent [before my enemies],
I refrained even from good,
And my distress grew worse.
AMP.3:
My heart was hot within me.
While I was musing the fire burned;
Then I spoke with my tongue:
AMP.4:
"LORD, let me know my [life's] end
And [to appreciate] the extent of my days;
Let me know how frail I am [how transient is my stay here].
AMP.5:
"Behold, You have made my days as [short as] hand widths,
And my lifetime is as nothing in Your sight.
Surely every man at his best is a mere breath [a wisp of smoke, a vapor that vanishes]! Selah.
AMP.6:
"Surely every man walks around like a shadow [in a charade];
Surely they make an uproar for nothing;
Each one builds up riches, not knowing who will receive them.
AMP.7:
"And now, Lord, for what do I expectantly wait?
My hope [my confident expectation] is in You.
AMP.8:
"Save me from all my transgressions;
Do not make me the scorn and reproach of the [self-righteous, arrogant] fool.
AMP.9:
"I am mute, I do not open my mouth,
Because it is You who has done it.
AMP.10:
"Remove Your plague from me;
I am wasting away because of the conflict and opposition of Your hand.
AMP.11:
"With rebukes You discipline man for sin;
You consume like a moth what is precious to him;
Surely every man is a mere breath [a wisp of smoke, a vapor that vanishes]. Selah.
AMP.12:
"Hear my prayer, O LORD, and listen to my cry;
Do not be silent at my tears;
For I am Your temporary guest,
A sojourner like all my fathers.
AMP.13:
"O look away from me, that I may smile and again know joy
Before I depart and am no more."

MSG.1:
A David psalm
I'm determined to watch steps and tongue
so they won't land me in trouble.
I decided to hold my tongue
as long as Wicked is in the room.
MSG.2:
"Mum's the word," I said, and kept quiet.
But the longer I kept silence
The worse it got—
MSG.3:
my insides got hotter and hotter.
My thoughts boiled over;
I spilled my guts.
MSG.4:
"Tell me, what's going on, God?
How long do I have to live?
Give me the bad news!
MSG.5:
You've kept me on pretty short rations;
my life is string too short to be saved.
MSG.6:
Oh! we're all puffs of air.
Oh! we're all shadows in a campfire.
Oh! we're just spit in the wind.
We make our pile, and then we leave it.
MSG.7:
"What am I doing in the meantime, Lord?
Hoping, that's what I'm doing—hoping
MSG.8:
You'll save me from a rebel life,
save me from the contempt of dunces.
MSG.9:
I'll say no more, I'll shut my mouth,
since you, Lord, are behind all this.
MSG.10:
But I can't take it much longer.
When you put us through the fire
MSG.11:
to purge us from our sin,
our dearest idols go up in smoke.
Are we also nothing but smoke?
MSG.12:
"Ah, God, listen to my prayer, my
cry—open your ears.
Don't be callous;
just look at these tears of mine.
I'm a stranger here. I don't know my way—
a migrant like my whole family.
MSG.13:
Give me a break, cut me some slack
before it's too late and I'm out of here."

NIV.1:
Psalm 39
For the director of music. For Jeduthun. A psalm of David.
I said, “I will watch my ways
and keep my tongue from sin;
I will put a muzzle on my mouth
as long as the wicked are in my presence.”
NIV.2:
But when I was silent and still,
not even saying anything good,
my anguish increased.
NIV.3:
My heart grew hot within me,
and as I meditated, the fire burned;
then I spoke with my tongue:
NIV.4:
“Show me, O Lord, my life’s end
and the number of my days;
let me know how fleeting is my life.
NIV.5:
You have made my days a mere handbreadth;
the span of my years is as nothing before you.
Each man’s life is but a breath. Selah
NIV.6:
Man is a mere phantom as he goes to and fro:
He bustles about, but only in vain;
he heaps up wealth, not knowing who will get it.
NIV.7:
“But now, Lord, what do I look for?
My hope is in you.
NIV.8:
Save me from all my transgressions;
do not make me the scorn of fools.
NIV.9:
I was silent; I would not open my mouth,
for you are the one who has done this.
NIV.10:
Remove your scourge from me;
I am overcome by the blow of your hand.
NIV.11:
You rebuke and discipline men for their sin;
you consume their wealth like a moth—
each man is but a breath. Selah
NIV.12:
“Hear my prayer, O Lord,
listen to my cry for help;
be not deaf to my weeping.
For I dwell with you as an alien,
a stranger, as all my fathers were.
NIV.13:
Look away from me, that I may rejoice again
before I depart and am no more.”

NRSVCE.1:
I said, “I will guard my ways
that I may not sin with my tongue;
I will keep a muzzle on my mouth
as long as the wicked are in my presence.”
NRSVCE.2:
I was silent and still;
I held my peace to no avail;
my distress grew worse,
NRSVCE.3:
my heart became hot within me.
While I mused, the fire burned;
then I spoke with my tongue:
NRSVCE.4:
“LORD, let me know my end,
and what is the measure of my days;
let me know how fleeting my life is.
NRSVCE.5:
You have made my days a few handbreadths,
and my lifetime is as nothing in your sight.
Surely everyone stands as a mere breath. Selah
NRSVCE.6:
Surely everyone goes about like a shadow.
Surely for nothing they are in turmoil;
they heap up, and do not know who will gather.
NRSVCE.7:
“And now, O Lord, what do I wait for?
My hope is in you.
NRSVCE.8:
Deliver me from all my transgressions.
Do not make me the scorn of the fool.
NRSVCE.9:
I am silent; I do not open my mouth,
for it is you who have done it.
NRSVCE.10:
Remove your stroke from me;
I am worn down by the blows of your hand.
NRSVCE.11:
“You chastise mortals
in punishment for sin,
consuming like a moth what is dear to them;
surely everyone is a mere breath. Selah
NRSVCE.12:
“Hear my prayer, O LORD,
and give ear to my cry;
do not hold your peace at my tears.
For I am your passing guest,
an alien, like all my forebears.
NRSVCE.13:
Turn your gaze away from me, that I may smile again,
before I depart and am no more.”

NET.1: I decided, “I will watch what I say and make sure I do not sin with my tongue. I will put a muzzle over my mouth while in the presence of an evil man.”
NET.2: I was stone silent; I held back the urge to speak. My frustration grew;
NET.3: my anxiety intensified. As I thought about it, I became impatient. Finally I spoke these words:
NET.4: “O LORD, help me understand my mortality and the brevity of life! Let me realize how quickly my life will pass!
NET.5: Look, you make my days short-lived, and my life span is nothing from your perspective. Surely all people, even those who seem secure, are nothing but vapor.
NET.6: Surely people go through life as mere ghosts. Surely they accumulate worthless wealth without knowing who will eventually haul it away.”
NET.7: But now, O Lord, upon what am I relying? You are my only hope!
NET.8: Deliver me from all my sins of rebellion! Do not make me the object of fools’ insults!
NET.9: I am silent and cannot open my mouth because of what you have done.
NET.10: Please stop wounding me! You have almost beaten me to death!
NET.11: You severely discipline people for their sins; like a moth you slowly devour their strength. Surely all people are a mere vapor. (Selah)
NET.12: Hear my prayer, O LORD! Listen to my cry for help! Do not ignore my sobbing! For I am dependent on you, like one residing outside his native land; I am at your mercy, just as all my ancestors were.
NET.13: Turn your angry gaze away from me, so I can be happy before I pass away.
